title: Workflow Guardrails Alignment
objective: Align scripts and docs on when a slice is allowed to start, how the documentation PR precedes the spec PR, and how slice aliases are derived.
description: The current workflow says no slice should enter execution until it is `ready`, but `start-slice.sh` still allows `draft` slices. The docs also assume a documentation-PR-before-spec-PR order and a branch strategy that are not surfaced consistently at the point of use. This slice makes the workflow contract explicit and enforceable.
must: Make `start-slice.sh` require `status=ready` by default, or provide an explicit escape hatch for intentional draft bootstraps
Align the onboarding docs with the actual order of operations: documentation PR first, then slice execution, then spec PR
Make alias generation deterministic and human-readable for both tickets such as `QUIVER-01` and simpler tickets such as `DEM-001`
Keep `WORKTREE_CONTEXT.md` and `ACTIVE_SLICES.md` consistent with the final alias and status model
acceptance: A `draft` slice does not silently enter execution through `start-slice.sh` without an explicit override
README, AI docs, and generated workflow docs describe the same execution order and branch assumptions
Alias output in worktree-related artifacts is readable and stable for both framework tickets and generic team tickets
tests: Alias generation snapshot for representative ticket formats
assumptions: The ready gate should be enforced at the bootstrap entrypoint, not only in a separate validation command
The documentation PR continues to be the source of truth before slice execution starts
